title: Is Innodata's Largest Customer Expansion a 2026 Breakout Catalyst?
text: The article discusses Innodata Inc. (INOD) and its potential growth trajectory in 2026, centered on expansion with its largest customer. Main thesis: Management received verbal confirmation for additional expansion with its largest customer, signaling movement beyond pilot programs toward scaled deployments. Growth drivers: Six of eight major big-tech customers are forecasted to grow substantially in 2026. Budgets increasingly tied to AI training and evaluation spending rather than short-term experiments. Q3 2025 showed record revenue growth with margin expansion. Competitive context: The article compares Innodata against three major competitors Accenture (ACN), Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH), and Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH) noting that while these firms offer broader services, Innodata's specialized expertise in AI training data and evaluation workflows provides competitive differentiation. Risk factors: Customer concentration remains a concern, though management is diversifying through federal and sovereign AI market expansion. Outlook: The expansion could demonstrate that Innodata's platform-driven model is entering a new phase of scalable, profitable growth.
